placeholders.intro_markdown:
# VibeVoice-ASR Colab Notebook

This notebook transcribes audio or video files.

## Features

- **Free to use:** This notebook uses [VibeVoice-ASR](https://github.com/microsoft/VibeVoice), which Microsoft open-sourced on January 21, 2026, together with Google Colab's free GPU runtime. To make the model usable on free GPUs, this notebook uses a quantized VibeVoice-ASR model.
- **No fixed notebook-side file size limit:** Large audio and video files can be processed by splitting them into segments.
- **Batch processing:** Multiple audio and video files can be processed in one run.
- **Google Drive support:** Files can be uploaded directly, or audio/video files stored in Google Drive can be processed.
- **Fully online:** Downloads and processing are performed on Google Colab. No local computing resources are used.
- **Multiple output formats:** The notebook outputs the original JSON transcript and derived TXT, CSV, Markdown, Excel, and SRT subtitle files.

## How to use

1. In **1. Settings**, choose `INPUT_MODE`. Edit the other settings if necessary.
2. Select **Runtime -> Run all**.
3. If `INPUT_MODE = "upload"`, wait until **2. File selection / Google Drive mount** starts, then click **Choose Files** in that cell's output area and upload your file(s).
4. When processing is complete, the transcribed files are downloaded automatically.

## Notes

- Google Colab's free GPU has usage limits. After a certain amount of use, GPU access may be temporarily restricted and restored later. If no GPU is available, this notebook stops before transcription.
- Processing takes time. Depending on Colab availability, it may take around 10-20 minutes before the notebook is ready to process files. As a rough guide, a 60-minute audio or video file may take about 1 hour to transcribe.
- Keep this notebook open while processing. If you close the notebook during transcription, processing stops.

placeholders.settings.input_mode_help: # @markdown - `INPUT_MODE`: Choose how to provide audio/video files. Use `upload` for local files, or `google_drive` for files in Google Drive.
placeholders.settings.multiple_files_comment: # Multiple files are allowed by default.
placeholders.settings.drive_only_help: # @markdown These settings are used only when `INPUT_MODE == "google_drive"`.
placeholders.settings.drive_subpath_help: # @markdown - `GOOGLE_DRIVE_SUBPATH`: A file or folder path under MyDrive. Leave blank to use the top-level folder of your Google Drive MyDrive.
placeholders.settings.recursive_help: # @markdown - `RECURSIVE`: If `GOOGLE_DRIVE_SUBPATH` points to a folder, also search its subfolders.
placeholders.settings.filename_contains_help: # @markdown - `FILENAME_CONTAINS`: Optional filename substring filter. Leave blank to disable filtering. This accepts a single text string only; it does not support multiple keywords or comma-separated values.
placeholders.settings.build_drive_path_comment: # Build Drive input path from root + relative subpath.
placeholders.settings.save_outputs_help: # @markdown - `SAVE_OUTPUTS_TO_GOOGLE_DRIVE`: Save final transcription output files to Google Drive. This is active only when `INPUT_MODE == "google_drive"`.
placeholders.settings.auto_download_help: # - `AUTO_DOWNLOAD_ZIP`: A ZIP file is created on Colab. If set to `False`, automatic download is skipped.
placeholders.settings.save_checkpoints_help: # - `SAVE_CHECKPOINTS_TO_GOOGLE_DRIVE`: If set to `True`, checkpoints are also saved to Google Drive. This lets you inspect partially generated files even if processing stops unexpectedly, but it causes frequent writes to Google Drive.
placeholders.settings.drive_output_root_help: # - `GOOGLE_DRIVE_OUTPUT_ROOT`: By default, a subfolder for saved outputs is created under the top-level folder of your Google Drive MyDrive.
placeholders.settings.segmentation_comment: # Audio is externally split into segments before ASR. Larger segments preserve more context but need more memory and time.
placeholders.settings.max_new_tokens_comment: # `MAX_NEW_TOKENS` limits the generated transcript JSON per segment.
placeholders.settings.acoustic_chunk_comment: # Internal acoustic tokenizer processing chunk size in audio samples, not seconds. Default 1440000 = 60 seconds at 24 kHz.
placeholders.settings.context_help: # @markdown - `CONTEXT_INFO`: Optional context used as a reference during transcription, such as keywords, names, organizations, and technical terms. Leave blank if unnecessary. Multiple keywords are supported; separate them with commas.
placeholders.settings.generation_comment: # These parameters control text generation after audio tokenization. The defaults are deterministic and ASR-oriented.
placeholders.settings.repetition_penalty_comment: # - `REPETITION_PENALTY=1.0` means no extra repetition penalty. Raise slightly only if repeated text appears.
placeholders.settings.other_params_comment: # Other parameters: TOP_K, LENGTH_PENALTY, EARLY_STOPPING, NO_REPEAT_NGRAM_SIZE, EOS_TOKEN_ID, PAD_TOKEN_ID, USE_CACHE, RETURN_DICT_IN_GENERATE, OUTPUT_SCORES
placeholders.settings.advanced_comment: # Advanced model settings are intentionally not form parameters for ordinary users.
placeholders.settings.base_model_comment: # - BASE_MODEL_ID: HF Transformers version of VibeVoice-ASR used as the source model.
placeholders.settings.load_strategy_comment: # - MODEL_LOAD_STRATEGY: "base_selective_nf4" loads BASE_MODEL_ID with selective NF4 quantization.
placeholders.settings.dubedo_model_comment: # - DUBEDO_MODEL_ID: pre-quantized model kept only as a reference; direct loading previously caused acoustic tokenizer quantization issues in some environments.
placeholders.settings.attn_comment: # - ATTN_IMPLEMENTATION: use "eager" because VibeVoiceAcousticTokenizerEncoderModel currently does not support SDPA in this Transformers path.
placeholders.settings.flash_attention_comment: # - INSTALL_FLASH_ATTENTION: installing flash-attn can take time or fail on Colab, so the default is False.
placeholders.settings.stream_output_comment: # - STREAM_OUTPUT: when True, generated text is streamed in the output area while each segment is being processed.
placeholders.settings.runtime_dirs_comment: # Runtime directories. Ordinary users normally do not need to edit these.
placeholders.settings.protected_modules_comment: # Modules that must remain unquantized. Quantizing these can break the acoustic tokenizer path.
placeholders.messages.choose_files: Click 'Choose Files' and upload one or more audio/video files.
placeholders.errors.no_supported_files: No supported audio/video files were selected.
placeholders.errors.gpu_runtime_full: This notebook is configured to use GPU. In Colab, select Runtime > Change runtime type > Hardware accelerator > GPU, then restart and rerun.
placeholders.messages.dependencies_done: Dependency installation step finished. If imports fail later, Runtime > Restart runtime and run cells again.
placeholders.model.liger_comment: # Optional Liger Kernel, as in the official demo. This affects Qwen2 components when supported by the runtime.
placeholders.comments.no_torch_reinstall: Do not reinstall torch here; use the Colab runtime's CUDA-enabled torch build.
placeholders.comments.flash_attn_compile: May compile for a long time or fail depending on the Colab image.
placeholders.comments.sdpa_failure: In this HF selective-NF4 path, the acoustic tokenizer encoder currently fails with SDPA.
placeholders.comments.eager_fallback: Therefore, even "auto" falls back to "eager" unless flash_attention_2 is explicitly available and selected.
placeholders.comments.model_input_device: For device_map={"": 0}, this is cuda:0. This also works if the first parameter is on CUDA.
placeholders.comments.bnb_skip_modules: Despite the name, this is also used by BitsAndBytesConfig to skip modules during quantized loading.
placeholders.comments.batch_encoding_to: BatchEncoding.to(device, dtype) keeps integer tensors integer and casts floating tensors.
placeholders.comments.official_post_process: Official post_process_transcription returns keys such as start_time/end_time/speaker_id/text.
placeholders.comments.output_roots: Store intermediate checkpoints and final outputs separately.
placeholders.comments.zip_uses_outputs: The ZIP download cell uses only the local outputs_* folder.
placeholders.comments.keep_raw_output: Keep the raw output visible even when structured parsing fails.
placeholders.comments.save_checkpoint: Save a checkpoint after every segment in the separate checkpoint folder.
placeholders.comments.save_final_outputs: Save final outputs locally. This folder is what the ZIP cell downloads.
placeholders.comments.zip_cell_intro: This cell creates a ZIP archive on Colab from the local outputs_* folder created by cell 7.
placeholders.comments.zip_not_drive: The ZIP file itself is not written to Google Drive.
placeholders.comments.checkpoints_excluded: Checkpoint files are stored in a separate timestamped folder and are not included.
placeholders.comments.prefer_runtime_output: Prefer the output folder created in the current runtime by cell 7.
placeholders.comments.fallback_latest_output: If the variable is not available, fall back to the latest outputs_* folder under OUTPUT_DIR.
placeholders.comments.preserve_zip_root: Preserve the outputs_* top-level folder inside the ZIP.

readme.template_note:
The template notebook is the source notebook and contains placeholders such as
`{{settings.input_mode_help}}`. Locale files provide language-specific values
for Colab-facing copy such as `# @title`, `# @markdown`, comments, and selected
user messages. Every locale contains the complete placeholder set from the
English source locale.
readme.generated_note: Generated notebooks are committed so Colab links stay stable.
readme.refresh_note:
`translate_full_locales.py` machine-translates the full English locale into the
other locale files while preserving code tokens such as `INPUT_MODE`,
`Google Drive`, `# @markdown`, and model identifiers. Review translated copy
before a formal release.
readme.checks_note:
The CI workflow checks that generated notebooks are up to date, have no saved
cell outputs, parse as JSON, contain no unresolved placeholders, and that code
cells are valid Python syntax.
readme.ci_intro: This repository can test the notebook as a distributable artifact:
ci_items: generation from the template and locale files
committed generated notebooks matching the template
readme.ci_limit:
The full transcription path requires Colab-specific APIs, large model downloads,
GPU availability, and user-provided media files. That should be treated as a
manual smoke test or a separate GPU/Colab validation workflow.
readme.license_note:
This repository's notebook scaffolding and build scripts are released under the
MIT License. VibeVoice-ASR, model weights, and third-party dependencies are
governed by their respective licenses.
